title: Execute the security, privacy, and prompt-injection threat suite

commands.name: Production dependency audit detected two high-severity advisories
commands.expectedFailure: The inherited graph contained fast-uri 3.1.3 (GHSA-v2hh-gcrm-f6hx) and optional sharp 0.34.5 (GHSA-f88m-g3jw-g9cj), both in known high-severity vulnerable ranges

commands.name: Frozen dependency graph, advisory regression, and production audit
commands.command: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; pnpm test:security; pnpm audit --prod

commands.name: Optimized production build with patched transitive dependencies
Complete repository gate recognized the new security evidence inventory
commands.expectedFailure: Traceability correctly reported 25 manifests and 263 proof files while the explicit inventory regression still expected the previous 24 manifests and 261 proof files

commands.name: Updated explicit evidence inventory and traceability
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm evidence:check

propertyTests.rationale: This prerequisite pins two finite advisory version ranges; generated inputs do not add assurance beyond the exact lockfile and audit assertions. The full SEC-01 boundary properties remain required before task completion.

bdd.rationale: Dependency resolution has no user-visible workflow. SEC-01 adversarial browser scenarios remain required before task completion.

visualEvidence.rationale: This prerequisite changes only the resolved production dependency graph and security regression. It changes no UI, browser state, finding, receipt, or recovery screen.

proofs.description: Permanent lockfile regression rejecting the two vulnerable transitive ranges and requiring their patched resolutions
Sanitized red/green dependency-audit record, compatibility result, applicability decisions, and partial-task boundary

knownLimitations: This is a narrow SEC-01 prerequisite, not completion of the threat suite; prompt injection, cross-workspace access, tampering, retention, publication, and safe-recovery cases remain outstanding.
The production audit reports advisories known to the configured package registry at run time; a clean result does not prove that the dependency graph is vulnerability-free.
The sharp override is compatible with the current optimized Next.js build on Windows; clean-checkout Ubuntu and Windows CI are still pending.
No product safety, compliance, approval, or real-world effectiveness claim follows from a clean dependency audit.
